methods.method_name: Superplasticizer Saturation Content Determination by Rheometry
methods.description: Determination of the optimal superplasticizer content for cement pastes by measuring yield stress using rotational rheometry.

steps.description: for the determination of superplasticizer saturation ratio, pastes with 0.3 water/cement were produced.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare cement pastes with a 0.3 water/cement ratio and varying superplasticizer content.
outputs: Cement paste samples with varying superplasticizer content

steps.description: The mixture of the pastes occurred in three steps: i) adding water in the powder in 5 s; ii) manual mixing with scoop for 50 s; and iii) dispersion in 10000 rpm rotation mixer for 1.5 min.
steps.searchable_summary: Mix cement pastes using a specific three-step procedure.

steps.description: Immediately after mixing, the pastes were subjected to rotational rheometry (Rheometer MARS 60, Haake), using plate-plate geometry with 35 mm in diameter and gap of 1 mm.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform rotational rheometry on fresh cement pastes using plate-plate geometry.
outputs: Rheological data (shear stress vs shear rate)

steps.description: The shear program used was the stepped flow test in two cycles of acceleration and deceleration, varying the shear rate from 0 to 50 s -1 .
steps.searchable_summary: Configure rheometer shear program as a stepped flow test (0-50 s⁻¹, 2 cycles).
inputs: Desired shear program parameters (stepped flow, 2 cycles, 0-50 s⁻¹)

steps.description: The first cycle was run only for paste normalization (structural breakdown),
steps.searchable_summary: Condition the paste sample using the first rheometer shear cycle.

steps.description: while the second cycle was considered for data collection.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect rheological data during the second shear cycle.
outputs: Rheological data (shear stress vs shear rate)

steps.description: At this stage, for each superplasticizer content yield stress was obtained in shear rate of 0 s -1 .
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate yield stress from rheological data (extrapolated to 0 s⁻¹ shear rate).
inputs: Rheological data (shear stress vs shear rate)
outputs: Yield stress values for each superplasticizer content

steps.description: The saturation content was defined as the posterior point to stabilization of the yield stress.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine superplasticizer saturation content based on the point where yield stress stabilizes.
inputs: Yield stress values vs superplasticizer content

methods.description: Measurement of cement reactivity by quantifying chemically combined water and portlandite content at different hydration ages using thermogravimetric analysis (TGA).

steps.description: Pastes with water/cement ratio of 0.5 were mixed in the same manner as described above,
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare cement pastes with a 0.5 water/cement ratio using the specified mixing procedure.
inputs: Mixing procedure details (from Superplasticizer Saturation method)

steps.description: then they were placed in cylindrical molds of 2.5 cm in diameter and 8 cm in height
steps.searchable_summary: Mold cement pastes into cylindrical specimens (2.5cm dia, 8cm height).

steps.description: and stored at 23 °C and 90-100% relative humidity.
steps.searchable_summary: Cure molded specimens at 23°C and 90-100% relative humidity.

steps.description: After 24 h, they were relocated to larger containers (3 cm in diameter and 10 cm in height) filled with deionized water, sealed and stored at 23 °C until the age of interest.
steps.searchable_summary: After 24h, transfer specimens to sealed containers with deionized water and store at 23°C until test age (7, 28, 91 days).

steps.description: Slices from the cylindrical specimens were removed with 2 mm of thickness
steps.searchable_summary: Cut 2mm thick slices from cured cylindrical specimens.

steps.description: and, then, underwent to three immersion cycles in isopropanol, the first two immersions of 1 h each and the third immersion of 24 h.
steps.searchable_summary: Stop hydration by immersing slices in isopropanol (3 cycles: 1h, 1h, 24h).

steps.description: Finally, the isopropanol was exchanged for diethyl ether, with the samples immersed for 16 h,
steps.searchable_summary: Exchange isopropanol with diethyl ether by immersing samples for 16 hours.

steps.description: and subsequently dried in chamber at 50 °C for 30 min.
steps.searchable_summary: Dry samples in a chamber at 50°C for 30 minutes.

steps.description: After the hydration stoppage, samples were ground
steps.searchable_summary: Grind dried samples into powder.

steps.description: and then subjected to thermogravimetric test (TGA, STA 409PC/PG, Netzsch).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform TGA on ground powder samples.

steps.description: The quantification of the chemically bound water (B w ) and the portlandite content (CH) were determined with the following equations [22]: where M is the molar mass, W x is the percentage of mass loss at temperature x (°C) and LOI is loss on ignition.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate chemically bound water and portlandite content from TGA data using specified equations.
